summary: Convert the rust-diffforge Tauri WebView frontend from vanilla JavaScript and CSS to React with styled-components and styled-icons, matching the Next.js project's UI implementation pattern.

review_items.summary: The desktop WebView frontend becomes a Vite React app instead of static HTML plus imperative DOM event wiring.
The placeholder sign-in and backend status behaviors are preserved but managed through React component state.
Regular CSS is replaced by styled-components, with global reset/body styling supplied by createGlobalStyle.
The desktop UI may use styled-icons for button and status affordances, following next-diffforge's dependency pattern.
No database schema, migration, SQL policy, or data access change is required.
The desktop app continues to consume the same hosted /api/hello connectivity endpoint without changing the producer contract.

patches.summary: Add React, styled-components, styled-icons, and the Vite React plugin dependencies.
Enable the Vite React plugin while preserving the existing Tauri development server settings.
Reduce static markup to the React mount node.
Replace imperative DOM behavior with a React app rendered into the Vite root.
Add the React login shell with styled-components and styled-icons.
Remove regular CSS after moving visual styling into styled-components.

compatibility.notes: The visible desktop preview and backend connectivity check stay equivalent. The change affects frontend implementation and bundle dependencies only.

tests.summary: Install and lock React, styled-components, styled-icons, and Vite React plugin dependencies.
Validate the React Vite frontend build.
